title: Lazarum russell-smithii
intro: Lazarum russell-smithii, also known as Russell's Lazarum, is a perennial plant species in the family Berberidaceae, native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. The bioactive constituents of Russell's Lazarum include alkaloids, such as berberine and palmitine, as well as other compounds like sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Russell's Lazarum is used in herbal medicine, and the most common preparations include extracts, tinctures, and teas, which are used to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and infections.
